memoryArtifacts.headline: Cotton of Etwall in 'A Genealogical and Heraldic Dictionary of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain, Vol 4 Part 1' by Bernard Burke
memoryArtifacts.description: A limited genealogy of the Cotton family of Etwall in 'A Genealogical and Heraldic Dictionary of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain' Vol. 4 Part 1, page 296; by Bernard Burke. This information is likely to be accurate, but should be supported with documentation.

zh.Coton, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾可托
zh.Alveley, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾阿爾維勒
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh de Cotton於1315年出生在英國史洛普爾可托時，他的父親Alan de Cotton26歲，母親Margaret de Acton21歲。他於1331年與Isabel Heaton在英國史洛普爾阿爾維勒結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1360年在英國爾盧德亞斯享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh de Cotton于1315年出生在英国史洛普爾可托时，他的父亲Alan de Cotton26岁，母亲Margaret de Acton21岁。他于1331年与Isabel Heaton在英国史洛普爾阿爾維勒结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1360年在英国爾盧德亞斯享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cotton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called from Old English (æt) cotum (dative plural of cot) ‘at the cottages or huts’ (or sometimes possibly from a Middle English plural, coten). Examples include Coton (Cambridgeshire, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire), Cottam (East Yorkshire, Lancashire, Nottinghamshire), and Cotham (Nottinghamshire). Particularly common in Staffordshire.
French: from a pet form of the personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ), derived via Nicot, or of the personal name Jacques , derived via Jacot.
History: John Cotton (1584–1652) was a noted Puritan preacher, who landed at Boston, MA, from London in 1633 and became leader of the Congregationalists in America.

variants.name: Cotten
meanings: Altered form of German Kotten, a habitational name from any of several places so named in Rhineland, Westphalia, Silesia, etc., or perhaps an American shortened form of composite German surnames such as Kottenhagen, Kottenhoff, Kottenkamp (see Koth ).

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Cotham
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Cotton  .

variants.name: Cottam
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): variant of   Cotton  .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh de Cotton was born in 1315, in Coton, Shropshire, England, his father, Alan de Cotton, was 26 and his mother, Margaret de Acton, was 21. He married Isabel Heaton in 1331, in Alveley, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1360, in Rudheath, Cheshire, England, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh de Cotton est né en 1315, à Coton, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Alan de Cotton, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Acton, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Heaton en 1331, à Alveley, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1360, à Rudheath, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh de Cotton 1315 in Coton, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alan de Cotton 26 und seine Mutter Margaret de Acton 21. Er hat 1331 in Alveley, Shropshire, England Isabel Heaton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1360 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Rudheath, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh de Cotton è nato il nel 1315 nel Coton, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Alan de Cotton, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Acton, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Isabel Heaton nel 1331, nel Alveley, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1360, nel Rudheath, Cheshire, England, all'età di 45 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Cottonが1315年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアコトンで生まれた時，父Alan de Cottonは26歳，母Margaret de Actonは21歳であった。1331年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアアルベリーでIsabel Heatonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1360年にイギリスチェシュアイアルドヒースで45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Cotton(이)가 1315년 영국 슈럽실르 컷에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan de Cotton(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Acton(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1331년 영국 슈럽실르 앨벨리에서 Isabel Heaton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1360년 영국 체실르 루드히스에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh de Cotton nasceu em 1315, em Coton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alan de Cotton, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Acton, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Heaton em 1331, em Alveley, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1360, em Rudheath, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh de Cotton родился в 1315 г. в местности котон, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alan de Cotton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Acton, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Heaton в 1331 г. в местности алвели, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1360 г. в местности рудхит, чешир, Англия в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh de Cotton nació en 1315, en Coton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Alan de Cotton, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margaret de Acton, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Isabel Heaton en 1331, en Alveley, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1360, en Rudheath, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 45 años.
